question: what is the total purchase order value
business_context: purchase order value comes from OPOR and represents committed purchase amount, not invoiced liability
embedding_text: total purchase order value total po amount purchase commitment ordered value sum orders
sql: SELECT SUM("DocTotal") AS "total_purchase_order_value" FROM opor WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y'

question: how many purchase orders are open
business_context: open purchase orders are OPOR rows where docstatus is O and the document is not cancelled
embedding_text: count open purchase orders how many pending po open po pending orders
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "open_purchase_order_count" FROM opor WHERE "DocStatus" = 'O' AND COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y'

question: which vendors have the highest purchase order value
business_context: group purchase order committed value by vendor from OPOR
embedding_text: top vendors by purchase order value supplier wise po amount vendor spend ordered value ranking
sql: SELECT "CardCode", "CardName", SUM("DocTotal") AS "total_order_value" FROM opor WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' GROUP BY "CardCode", "CardName" ORDER BY "total_order_value" DESC LIMIT 10

question: which items have the most pending purchase order quantity
business_context: pending purchase quantity comes from POR1 openqty for open purchase order lines
embedding_text: top pending items purchase order open quantity item wise pending po quantity not received
sql: SELECT p1."ItemCode", p1."Dscription", SUM(p1."OpenQty") AS "pending_quantity" FROM por1 p1 JOIN opor p0 ON p0."DocEntry" = p1."DocEntry" WHERE p0."DocStatus" = 'O' AND COALESCE(p0."CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' GROUP BY p1."ItemCode", p1."Dscription" ORDER BY "pending_quantity" DESC LIMIT 10

question: show overdue purchase orders
business_context: overdue purchase orders are open non-cancelled OPOR rows with docduedate before current date
embedding_text: overdue purchase orders delayed po due date passed open orders vendor delivery delay
sql: SELECT "DocNum", "CardCode", "CardName", "DocDueDate", "DocTotal" FROM opor WHERE "DocStatus" = 'O' AND COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' AND "DocDueDate" < CURRENT_DATE ORDER BY "DocDueDate" ASC LIMIT 10

question: what is the total AP invoice amount
business_context: AP invoice value comes from OPCH and represents actual vendor liability
embedding_text: total ap invoice amount total purchase invoice value vendor bill payable liability sum invoices
sql: SELECT SUM("DocTotal") AS "total_ap_invoice_amount" FROM opch WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y'

question: how much AP invoice balance is due
business_context: balance due is the unpaid payable amount on OPCH, derived as DocTotal minus PaidToDate
embedding_text: ap invoice balance due unpaid payable outstanding vendor bills pending payment
sql: SELECT SUM("DocTotal" - IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0)) AS "total_balance_due" FROM opch WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y'

question: show me all open AP invoices with pending amounts
intent: list open AP invoices with pending amount
business_context: open AP invoices are OPCH rows with DocStatus O and active cancellation status; pending amount is DocTotal minus PaidToDate
embedding_text: show list open ap invoices pending amount balance due outstanding unpaid vendor bills payable
sql: SELECT "DocEntry", "DocNum", "CardCode", "CardName", "DocDate", "DocDueDate", "DocTotal", "PaidToDate", ("DocTotal" - IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0)) AS "pending_amount", "DocStatus" FROM opch WHERE "DocStatus" = 'O' AND COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' ORDER BY "DocDueDate" ASC LIMIT 50

question: which vendors have the highest AP invoice amount
business_context: group actual invoiced vendor liability by vendor from OPCH
embedding_text: top vendors by ap invoice value supplier wise invoice amount vendor payable spend ranking
sql: SELECT "CardCode", "CardName", SUM("DocTotal") AS "total_invoice_value" FROM opch WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' GROUP BY "CardCode", "CardName" ORDER BY "total_invoice_value" DESC LIMIT 10

question: which items have the highest invoiced quantity
business_context: invoiced item quantity comes from PCH1 quantity joined to OPCH
embedding_text: top invoiced items purchase invoice quantity item wise billed quantity vendor bill lines
sql: SELECT p1."ItemCode", p1."Dscription", SUM(p1."Quantity") AS "invoiced_quantity" FROM pch1 p1 JOIN opch p0 ON p0."DocEntry" = p1."DocEntry" WHERE COALESCE(p0."CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' GROUP BY p1."ItemCode", p1."Dscription" ORDER BY "invoiced_quantity" DESC LIMIT 10

question: what is the total purchase return value
business_context: purchase return value comes from ORPD and represents goods returned to vendors
embedding_text: total purchase return value total vendor returns goods return amount sum returns
sql: SELECT SUM("DocTotal") AS "total_purchase_return_value" FROM orpd WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y'

question: which vendors have the highest purchase return amount
business_context: group purchase return value by vendor from ORPD
embedding_text: top vendors by purchase return value supplier returns vendor return amount ranking
sql: SELECT "CardCode", "CardName", SUM("DocTotal") AS "total_return_value" FROM orpd WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' GROUP BY "CardCode", "CardName" ORDER BY "total_return_value" DESC LIMIT 10

question: which items are returned the most
business_context: returned item quantity comes from RPD1 quantity joined to ORPD
embedding_text: top returned items purchase return quantity item wise returned quantity vendor returns
sql: SELECT r1."ItemCode", r1."Dscription", SUM(r1."Quantity") AS "returned_quantity" FROM rpd1 r1 JOIN orpd r0 ON r0."DocEntry" = r1."DocEntry" WHERE COALESCE(r0."CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' GROUP BY r1."ItemCode", r1."Dscription" ORDER BY "returned_quantity" DESC LIMIT 10

question: compare purchase order value and AP invoice value
intent: compare committed purchase order value with actual AP invoice value
business_context: purchase orders are commitments from OPOR, AP invoices are actual vendor liabilities from OPCH
embedding_text: compare purchase order value ap invoice value ordered vs invoiced commitment vs liability
sql: SELECT (SELECT SUM("DocTotal") FROM opor WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y') AS "total_purchase_order_value", (SELECT SUM("DocTotal") FROM opch WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y') AS "total_ap_invoice_value"

question: find AP invoices where invoice amount exceeds purchase order amount by more than 5 percent
intent: AP invoice versus purchase order value variance
business_context: invoice lines copied from purchase orders carry PCH1 BaseType 22, BaseEntry, and BaseLine; compare AP invoice line value with source PO line value to find overbilling
embedding_text: ap invoice purchase order amount variance exceeds po amount price variance overbilling invoice amount greater than po amount five percent
sql: SELECT p0."DocNum" AS "invoice_number", p0."CardCode", p0."CardName", o0."DocNum" AS "po_number", CAST(SUM(p1."LineTotal") AS DOUBLE) AS "invoice_line_total", CAST(SUM(o1."LineTotal") AS DOUBLE) AS "po_line_total", CAST(SUM(p1."LineTotal") - SUM(o1."LineTotal") AS DOUBLE) AS "variance_amount", CAST(((SUM(p1."LineTotal") - SUM(o1."LineTotal")) / NULLIF(SUM(o1."LineTotal"), 0)) * 100 AS DOUBLE) AS "variance_percent" FROM pch1 p1 JOIN opch p0 ON p0."DocEntry" = p1."DocEntry" JOIN por1 o1 ON o1."DocEntry" = p1."BaseEntry" AND o1."LineNum" = p1."BaseLine" JOIN opor o0 ON o0."DocEntry" = o1."DocEntry" WHERE p1."BaseType" = 22 AND COALESCE(p0."CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' GROUP BY p0."DocNum", p0."CardCode", p0."CardName", o0."DocNum" HAVING ((SUM(p1."LineTotal") - SUM(o1."LineTotal")) / NULLIF(SUM(o1."LineTotal"), 0)) > 0.05 ORDER BY "variance_percent" DESC LIMIT 50

question: show AP invoices blocked for payment where amount is still outstanding
intent: blocked AP invoices with unpaid payable balance
business_context: blocked vendor invoices use OPCH PayBlock and unpaid balance is derived from DocTotal minus PaidToDate
embedding_text: blocked payment ap invoices payment block vendor invoices outstanding payable balance unpaid blocked for payment
sql: SELECT "DocNum", "CardCode", "CardName", "DocDate", "DocDueDate", CAST("DocTotal" AS DOUBLE) AS "DocTotal", CAST("PaidToDate" AS DOUBLE) AS "PaidToDate", CAST(("DocTotal" - IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0)) AS DOUBLE) AS "BalanceDue", "PayBlock" FROM opch WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' AND "PayBlock" = 'Y' AND ("DocTotal" - IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0)) > 0 ORDER BY "DocDueDate" ASC LIMIT 50

question: list duplicate vendor invoices based on vendor reference, vendor and amount
business_context: possible duplicate vendor bills share vendor code, vendor reference number NumAtCard, and document total
embedding_text: duplicate vendor invoices same invoice number vendor reference numatcard vendor amount duplicate bills
sql: SELECT "CardCode", "CardName", "NumAtCard", CAST("DocTotal" AS DOUBLE) AS "DocTotal", COUNT(*) AS "invoice_count", MIN("DocDate") AS "first_invoice_date", MAX("DocDate") AS "last_invoice_date" FROM opch WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' AND "NumAtCard" IS NOT NULL GROUP BY "CardCode", "CardName", "NumAtCard", "DocTotal" HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY "invoice_count" DESC, "last_invoice_date" DESC LIMIT 50

question: generate AP invoice aging by vendor
intent: AP invoice aging buckets by vendor
business_context: aging uses open unpaid AP invoice balances grouped by vendor and bucketed by days past due
embedding_text: ap invoice aging vendor outstanding payable balance buckets 0 30 31 60 61 90 over 90 days overdue
sql: SELECT "CardCode", "CardName", CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN "DocDueDate" >= CURRENT_DATE THEN ("DocTotal" - IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0)) ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) AS "current_amount", CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN "DocDueDate" < CURRENT_DATE AND DAYS_BETWEEN("DocDueDate", CURRENT_DATE) BETWEEN 1 AND 30 THEN ("DocTotal" - IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0)) ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) AS "days_1_30", CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN DAYS_BETWEEN("DocDueDate", CURRENT_DATE) BETWEEN 31 AND 60 THEN ("DocTotal" - IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0)) ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) AS "days_31_60", CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN DAYS_BETWEEN("DocDueDate", CURRENT_DATE) BETWEEN 61 AND 90 THEN ("DocTotal" - IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0)) ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) AS "days_61_90", CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN DAYS_BETWEEN("DocDueDate", CURRENT_DATE) > 90 THEN ("DocTotal" - IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0)) ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) AS "days_over_90" FROM opch WHERE "DocStatus" = 'O' AND COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' AND ("DocTotal" - IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0)) > 0 GROUP BY "CardCode", "CardName" ORDER BY "days_over_90" DESC, "days_61_90" DESC LIMIT 50

question: find AP invoice tax code mismatches against purchase order lines
intent: AP invoice versus purchase order tax code mismatch
business_context: compare tax code on AP invoice lines with tax code on the source purchase order line
embedding_text: tax code mismatch ap invoice purchase order invoice tax code differs po tax code vendor bill tax variance
sql: SELECT p0."DocNum" AS "invoice_number", o0."DocNum" AS "po_number", p0."CardCode", p0."CardName", p1."LineNum" AS "invoice_line", p1."ItemCode", p1."TaxCode" AS "invoice_tax_code", o1."TaxCode" AS "po_tax_code" FROM pch1 p1 JOIN opch p0 ON p0."DocEntry" = p1."DocEntry" JOIN por1 o1 ON o1."DocEntry" = p1."BaseEntry" AND o1."LineNum" = p1."BaseLine" JOIN opor o0 ON o0."DocEntry" = o1."DocEntry" WHERE p1."BaseType" = 22 AND COALESCE(p0."CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' AND IFNULL(p1."TaxCode", '') <> IFNULL(o1."TaxCode", '') ORDER BY p0."DocDate" DESC LIMIT 50

question: show partially paid AP invoices overdue today
business_context: partially paid AP invoices have PaidToDate greater than zero but less than DocTotal, and overdue means DocDueDate is before current date
embedding_text: partially paid overdue ap invoices unpaid balance vendor invoices past due partial payment
sql: SELECT "DocNum", "CardCode", "CardName", "DocDueDate", CAST("DocTotal" AS DOUBLE) AS "DocTotal", CAST("PaidToDate" AS DOUBLE) AS "PaidToDate", CAST(("DocTotal" - IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0)) AS DOUBLE) AS "BalanceDue" FROM opch WHERE COALESCE("CANCELED", 'N') <> 'Y' AND "DocDueDate" < CURRENT_DATE AND IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0) > 0 AND IFNULL("PaidToDate", 0) < "DocTotal" ORDER BY "DocDueDate" ASC LIMIT 50
